threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest tube, but everyone else just nods and points to the buses.
Caught a man in a full tuxedo arguing with a bus driver about not having change—pure Brixton late night chaos. Taxi not an option, I guess.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I was home when the kebab shop ran out of bread and the owner just shrugged and tossed us some flatbreads instead. That's real Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you get a Döner after midnight while a busker plays accordion to your late-night cravings. It's a vibe you can't replicate.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to avoid Valencia during bus delays. While tourists wonder why the buses won't move, we just stroll down the alley.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading Muni riders with a ukulele at Civic Center, just after the whole station went haywire. Only in SF do we turn chaos into a concert.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just a pretty face, but the real charm is in our tiny, hidden bars with no signs. You'd miss them if you only follow Google maps.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newbie by their map obsession—always staring at their phones instead of just following the vibrant street art that guides the way.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just the 'Overground' when catching the gospel train from Hackney Central. It's a rite of passage.
Caught a 59 last night—saw two guys arguing over who could do a better impression of the driver. Their accents were spot on, almost more entertaining than the ride!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted that small café on the corner serving Turkish coffee at 6 AM, I realized I’d found my new home in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg will you find a bar that serves cocktails in old soup cans, complete with a side of pickled herring. Just another day in Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s a local when they don’t flinch at a loud bang in Civic Center but start checking their watch for BART delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped for over ten minutes because someone decided to try and board with a giant inflatable dinosaur. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the parks and cafés, but the real vibe hides in the tiny alleys where you’ll catch locals arguing over football.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones asking for directions to Park Güell like it’s a hidden gem, unaware it’s packed with tourists every day.
